title: docstrings of _check_daemonset_ready / _check_deployment_ready say BlockedStatus but code returns WaitingStatus
summary: In src/charm.py the two readiness helpers _check_daemonset_ready (line 273-274) and _check_deployment_ready (line 286-287) both carry the docstring "Check if a <X> is ready. Returns BlockedStatus if not ready, None otherwise." but the implementations actually return ops.WaitingStatus (lines 281 and 294), never BlockedStatus. The docstrings are misleading about the function contract.
why_good_first_issue: Pure docstring fix in two adjacent one-line docstrings; no behaviour change. The correct value is unambiguous and is already asserted by the existing unit test (tests/unit/test_status.py:25 expects ops.WaitingStatus). Done = both docstrings say WaitingStatus. No runtime/deploy needed; lint+unit already green.
affected_files: src/charm.py:274 (_check_daemonset_ready docstring); src/charm.py:287 (_check_deployment_ready docstring); evidence of intended behaviour: src/charm.py:281,294 (return ops.WaitingStatus) and tests/unit/test_status.py:25 (asserts ops.WaitingStatus)
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/istio-k8s-operator && sed -n "272,295p" src/charm.py' ; multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/istio-k8s-operator && sed -n "15,28p" tests/unit/test_status.py' ; multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/istio-k8s-operator && export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit'
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection + unit run. src/charm.py:274 docstring='Check if a DaemonSet is ready. Returns BlockedStatus if not ready, None otherwise.' yet src/charm.py:281 returns ops.WaitingStatus(message); same mismatch at src/charm.py:287 vs :294. The intended return is WaitingStatus, proven by tests/unit/test_status.py:25 'assert state.unit_status == ops.WaitingStatus("Istio CNI not ready. Possible platform mismatch. Check charm config.")'. tox -e unit PASSED (exit 0) and tox -e lint passed (All checks passed!), so docstring is the only defect. Observed: docstring says BlockedStatus; Expected: WaitingStatus.
fix_hint: In src/charm.py change both docstrings (lines 274 and 287) from 'Returns BlockedStatus if not ready' to 'Returns WaitingStatus if not ready'. Optionally tighten the return-type wording to match ops.StatusBase | None signature. No code/test change required.
notes: No existing open issue covers this (grepped all 22 open issues for docstring/BlockedStatus/WaitingStatus -> none). Trivial, mechanical, self-contained.

title: Missing RBAC permissions when deploying istio-k8s operator in the Charmed Kubernetes
summary: When deployed on Charmed Kubernetes, istio-k8s fails with lightkube ApiError: 'customresourcedefinitions.apiextensions.k8s.io is forbidden: User system:serviceaccount:istio-system:istio-k8s cannot list resource customresourcedefinitions ... at the cluster scope'. The charm uses lightkube to get/list/patch cluster-scoped resources (CustomResourceDefinition, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding are imported in src/charm.py:51,56-58) but the required ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding granting list/get/patch on customresourcedefinitions is not provisioned, so it works on MicroK8s (broad RBAC) but not on Charmed Kubernetes.
why_good_first_issue: Well-scoped and the reporter already identified the exact missing verbs (list, get, patch on customresourcedefinitions) and the fix shape (add ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding). A beginner can add the RBAC rules and validate against the error message. Slightly above trivial because it needs a Charmed-Kubernetes cluster to fully verify, hence integration tier.
affected_files: src/charm.py:51 (imports CustomResourceDefinition), src/charm.py:56-58,74-75 (ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding usage); charmcraft.yaml (charm-level RBAC / no cluster-scope grant for CRDs); src/manifests/gateway-apis-crds.yaml
repro_steps: PLAN (do NOT run on the live cos VM): 1) On a Charmed-Kubernetes substrate (NOT MicroK8s, whose default RBAC is permissive): juju add-model istio-system; juju deploy istio-k8s --trust --channel 2/edge. 2) Observe unit go to error; juju debug-log shows lightkube.core.exceptions.ApiError 'customresourcedefinitions.apiextensions.k8s.io is forbidden ... cannot list resource customresourcedefinitions ... at the cluster scope'. 3) Confirm fix: add ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding granting [list,get,patch] on apiextensions.k8s.io/customresourcedefinitions and re-deploy; unit should reach active.
repro_result: LIVE-ATTEMPTED 2026-06-16: NOT reproducible on microk8s (istio-k8s deployed without --trust still went active). Needs Charmed Kubernetes' stricter RBAC. || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Could not reproduce locally: MicroK8s on the shared cos VM has permissive RBAC and the repo is read-only; the bug only manifests on Charmed Kubernetes per the issue. Static evidence supporting the report: src/charm.py imports and operates on cluster-scoped CustomResourceDefinition (line 51) and ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding (lines 56-58, 74-75) via lightkube_client, matching the forbidden 'list customresourcedefinitions' error in the issue.
fix_hint: Add a ClusterRole (rules: apiGroups ['apiextensions.k8s.io'], resources ['customresourcedefinitions'], verbs ['get','list','patch']) plus a ClusterRoleBinding to the istio-k8s service account, provisioned by the charm (or documented as a required --trust/manifest). Cross-check other cluster-scoped resources the charm touches and grant matching verbs.
notes: Real open Type: Bug issue. Integration-escalation: requires a Charmed Kubernetes deployment that the shared live cos model must not be used for. Reporter pinned the exact missing verbs, which de-risks the fix.
